# Enhance `security` review with Cedar + Guardians — Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Add real Cedar (transcript tool-call audit) + Guardians (plan data-flow) engines to the
`security` review dimension as deterministic pre-agent steps; the agent folds LOCKED violations into
anchored critical findings → blocks via the existing triage gate.

**Architecture:** Engines run in `review-security-agent.md` `steps:` (which can `pip`/`npm install`);
results staged as `engine-report.json`; rubric directs the agent to incorporate them; post-step
records `engine_report` in evidence. No new stage/check/protocol.json change. See the sibling spec.

**Tech stack:** Node (Cedar `@cedar-policy/cedar-wasm`), Python3 (Guardians `metareflection/guardians`
+ `z3-solver`), gh-aw (`gh aw compile` for the lock). Tests: the protocol's plain-Python pattern.

## Global constraints
- Touch **only** the `security` dimension: `scripts/security/**`, `rubrics/security.md`,
  `review-security-agent.{md,lock.yml}`, one `tests/` file, the spec/plan. No `protocol.json`,
  `engine/`, `checks/`, or other-dimension changes.
- LOCKED guardrails (`locked.no-exfiltration`, `locked.no-destructive`; guardians `locked:true`
  rules) cannot be weakened by a repo's custom policy.
- Every engine step is **fail-open** (`|| true` / fallback JSON) — a missing transcript/plan/dep
  never fails the run.
- Custom policy is **data, never executed** (Cedar parsed by the engine; guardians YAML by
  `compile.py`). Never `exec`/`eval`/`import` a repo-supplied policy file.
- The deployed `review-security-agent.lock.yml` MUST be regenerated by `gh aw compile` (v0.77.5),
  not hand-edited.

---

## Task 1: Port the verified engine drivers + policy folder

**Files (copy from the upstream custody build, repath to `scripts/security/`):**
- `_cedar-decide.js`, `policy-merge.js`, `actions-from-transcript.js`, `run-cedar.js` (Cedar)
- `compile.py`, `verify_driver.py` (Guardians)
- `policy/cedar/schema.cedarschema`, `policy/cedar/default/*.cedar` (7 files), `policy/guardians/default.policy.yaml`
- `package.json` pinning `@cedar-policy/cedar-wasm`

- [ ] Copy the files verbatim into `.github/agent-factory/protocols/code-review/scripts/security/`.
- [ ] `run-cedar.js` already takes `<defaultDir> <customDir|""> <convDir> <changedPathsJson>` and reads `*.jsonl` from `convDir` — no change needed; it works on this repo's `.conversations` transcripts (same Claude Code `tool_use` format).
- [ ] Sanity-run locally on the existing test payloads to confirm parity (the Cedar exfil + destructive flags fire).
- [ ] Commit.

## Task 2: Deterministic plan→AST extractor (`plan-extract.js`)

**File:** `scripts/security/plan-extract.js`

**Contract:** `node plan-extract.js <plan.txt>` → stdout a Guardians Workflow AST
`{ "steps": [ { "tool", "args", "result"? } ] }` consumed by `verify_driver.py`.

- [ ] Implement a heuristic scanner over the plan markdown that emits AST nodes when it detects:
  - a **secret-read** signal (`.env`, `secrets`, `credential`, `*.key`) ⇒ `{tool:"read_secret", result:"s"}`
  - an **external-sink** signal (a URL / "POST"/"send to"/"upload"/"exfiltrate") ⇒ `{tool:"network_send", args:{host:<host|"external">, body:{"$ref":"s"}}}`
  - an **injected-instruction** signal (HTML-comment imperatives, "do not tell the user", "ignore previous instructions") ⇒ `{tool:"read_external", result:"instr"}` + `{tool:"run_command", args:{argv:{"$ref":"instr"}}}`
  - none of the above ⇒ `{ "steps": [] }`
- [ ] Keep it pure (no network, stdlib `fs` only). Document it as a v1 heuristic.
- [ ] Add a node smoke assertion (in the Task 5 test or a tiny inline check): injected fixture ⇒ non-empty AST with read_secret→network_send; benign fixture ⇒ empty steps.
- [ ] Commit.

## Task 3: `emit-engine-report.js`

**File:** `scripts/security/emit-engine-report.js`

**Contract:** `node emit-engine-report.js <cedar.json> <guardians.json>` → stdout
`{ "violations": [ { engine, name, severity, locked, evidence, ref } ], "summary": {critical, high, medium} }`
where each Cedar flag and each Guardians violation becomes one entry; severity = LOCKED⇒critical,
tunable⇒high, warning⇒medium. Tolerate missing/`n/a` inputs (→ empty violations).

- [ ] Implement (pure; reuse the severity logic from the upstream `emit-findings.js`).
- [ ] Commit.

## Task 3b: `anchor-engine-findings.js` — deterministic gate (no agent dependency)

**File:** `scripts/security/anchor-engine-findings.js`

**Contract:** `node anchor-engine-findings.js <engine-report.json> <pr.diff> <evidence.json>` — rewrites
`evidence.json` in place: for each **LOCKED** violation in the report, append a `findings[]` entry
`{ path, line, severity:"critical", category:"security", title, impact, fix }` **anchored to a real
RIGHT-side added line** parsed from `pr.diff` (so `review-findings-anchored` passes); if any added,
set `verdict:"REQUEST_CHANGES"`. Always record the full report under `engine_report`. If `evidence.json`
is missing/unreadable, start from `{dimension:"security",verdict:"APPROVE",findings:[]}` (so the engines
still produce valid evidence even if the agent died). If the diff has **no** added line, inject nothing
and set `engine_report.unanchored=true`.

- [ ] Implement the unified-diff parser (find the first `+++ b/<path>` + `@@ +<n>` hunk, advance the
  RIGHT line counter over context/added lines, return the first added line's `{path,line}`).
- [ ] Pure (stdlib `fs` only); tolerate missing inputs.
- [ ] Commit.

## Task 4: Wire the steps into `review-security-agent.md` + extend the rubric + recompile

**Files:** `.github/workflows/review-security-agent.md`, `rubrics/security.md`.

- [ ] Add a **`uses: actions/setup-python@v5`** step (`python-version: '3.11'`) before the engine step, so Guardians + Z3 run on the verified interpreter. The engine + post steps call **`python3.11`** explicitly (install: `python3.11 -m pip install --quiet "git+https://github.com/metareflection/guardians@main" z3-solver pydantic pyyaml || true`).
- [ ] In `review-security-agent.md` `steps:` (after the existing prefetch), add ONE engine step (all `|| true` / fallback):
  - `HEAD_SHA=$(jq -r .headRefOid pr.json)`; fetch transcripts: `node …/scripts/context/locate.js /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.json /tmp/gh-aw/agent/transcripts || true`
  - fetch the plan: derive plan path from `pr.json` files (regex `docs/.*plans?/|^plans?/|PLAN.md`), `gh api repos/$REPO/contents/<path>?ref=$HEAD_SHA --jq .content | base64 -d > /tmp/gh-aw/agent/plan.txt` (else empty)
  - fetch optional custom policy (`.custody/policy/cedar/*.cedar`, `.custody/policy/guardians.policy.yaml`) via `gh api` at `$HEAD_SHA` into temp dirs (tolerate 404)
  - install deps: `( cd <scripts/security> && npm install … ) ; python3.11 -m pip install … || true`
  - run Cedar → `cedar.json`; `plan-extract.js plan.txt` → `gx-workflow.json`; `python3.11 verify_driver.py` → `guardians.json`; `emit-engine-report.js` → `/tmp/gh-aw/agent/engine-report.json`
- [ ] Replace the record post-step with the **deterministic gate** post-step (BEFORE upload): `node …/scripts/security/anchor-engine-findings.js /tmp/gh-aw/agent/engine-report.json /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff /tmp/gh-aw/evidence.json` (injects anchored critical findings + records `engine_report`).
- [ ] In `rubrics/security.md`, add a short **"Engine report"** note: deterministic Cedar/Guardians LOCKED violations are added to the evidence automatically by a post-step — the agent should do its **own** code-level security review per the rubric and **not** duplicate engine findings. (No agent dependency for blocking.)
- [ ] Recompile: `gh aw compile .github/workflows/review-security-agent.md` → regenerates `.lock.yml`.
- [ ] Commit canonical `.md` + rubric + regenerated `.lock.yml` together.

## Task 5: Test + verify + PR

**File:** `tests/test_security_engines.py` (the repo's plain-Python subprocess pattern).

- [ ] Write fixtures: an injected plan (`.env` + external POST + injected comment) and a benign plan; an exfil transcript (`Read .env` → `WebFetch` external → `rm -rf`) and a benign transcript.
- [ ] Tests: `plan-extract.js` injected ⇒ AST has read_secret + network_send; benign ⇒ `steps:[]`. `verify_driver.py` on the injected AST ⇒ `ok:false` with LOCKED `no_secret_exfiltration`; clean AST ⇒ `ok:true`. Cedar (guarded): exfil transcript ⇒ `locked.no-exfiltration` + `locked.no-destructive`. `emit-engine-report.js` fuses both. **`anchor-engine-findings.js`: a LOCKED engine-report + a diff with an added line + a base evidence ⇒ a `critical` finding anchored to that line and `verdict:REQUEST_CHANGES`; a no-added-line diff ⇒ `engine_report.unanchored`.**
- [ ] Run the test locally with `python3.11` (full stack) AND system `python3` (guardians skips cleanly) → OK both.
- [ ] Verify end-to-end by running the full chain on the injection/unsafe payloads and confirm the LOCKED violations fire and `anchor-engine-findings.js` produces a blocking evidence.
- [ ] Commit. Push to `golivax2/yuanrong-datasystem` (updates the PR). PR notes: no new secret (default `GITHUB_TOKEN`); python pinned to 3.11; the remaining v1 limitations (heuristic plan-extractor; only LOCKED auto-gates; unanchored pure-deletion edge); live run is manual-acceptance.

## Self-review (run after implementing)
- Spec coverage: Cedar (T1), Guardians (T1–T2), engine report (T3), workflow+rubric+recompile (T4),
  tests+PR (T5). LOCKED-unweakenable, fail-open, custom-as-data: carried in the reused drivers.
- Only the security dimension is touched; `protocol.json`/`engine/`/`checks/` untouched.
- The lock is regenerated by `gh aw compile`, not hand-edited.
